Placements.generated_by: Adding the figures of the 9th and 10th Houses (the First and Second Nieces).
meaning: Past vector: how the querent arrived at the question
Inner stance: courage, denial, fatigue, or hunger for change
Root fuel: habit, debt, loyalty, or trauma still paying interest
Placements.utility_in_reading: Use the Right Witness to triage motive and history before you trust the Judge. It is the querent-side backstory lane—what they admit, what the chart admits for them.
Placements.essence: The dexter witness is the querent’s upstream weather: momentum, bias, and unfinished business that color the present tense. It rarely contradicts the First outright; it deepens it.
read_when: Career pivots explained by burnout years
Relationship charts where old attachment sets temperature
Money questions where spending habits predate the crisis
pitfalls: Assuming it predicts future instead of conditioning it
Ignoring when it matches First—then double-weight honesty
examples: Startup founder admits they already decided to sell—chart shows Via
Student frames exam question but Witness shows Carcer—study debt reality

Placements.generated_by: Adding the figures of the 11th and 12th Houses (the Third and Fourth Nieces).
meaning: Incoming vector: what presses against the querent now
Environmental tone: market, family, boss, or weather systems
Near-future hinge: offers, deadlines, rivals moving pieces
Placements.utility_in_reading: Weight the Left Witness against the Right: tension between history and horizon defines the drama. It is the world’s move on the querent’s chess square.
Placements.essence: The sinister witness names external leverage: partners, markets, regulators, lovers—forces that do not sleep in the querent’s skull but still push outcomes.
read_when: Hiring freeze hitting an offer letter
Partner’s visa timeline colliding with wedding
pitfalls: Reading it as secret enemy by default—sometimes it is help
Forgetting mobile figures change faster than stable ones
examples: Left Witness Acquisitio while Right Witness Amissio—cash window opens as pride drains
Populus left, Puer right—crowd noise egging rash move

Placements.generated_by: Adding the figures of the Right Witness (13) and Left Witness (14).
meaning: Verdict plane: yes / no / wait with teeth
Synthesis of both witnesses after elemental addition
Integrity check: even dots—if odd, recount mothers
Placements.utility_in_reading: The Judge is the compressed answer once past and near-future vectors have spoken. It obeys geomantic arithmetic—trust the math before the story.
Placements.essence: Judges crystallize the quarrel between witnesses into a single hue: benefic, malefic, mixed, or null. It is the headline, not the fine print.
pitfalls: Treating Judge as irrelevant if emotionally disliked
examples: Judge Fortuna Major with sentence Tristitia—win costs sleep
Judge Populus—outcome mirrors allies; check eleventh

Placements.generated_by: Adding the figure of the Judge (15) to the figure of the First House (1 - The First Mother).
meaning: Aftermath: how the verdict lands in the querent’s bones
Coping trajectory: relief, regret, or addiction to next crisis
Slow variables: lease, visa, therapy, vesting cliffs
Placements.utility_in_reading: The Reconciler (Sentence) narrates the second wave: feelings, finances, and health six months after the confetti. It saves charts from naive optimism.
Placements.essence: Sentence is the epilogue: whether the promotion feeds the marriage, whether the settlement cures insomnia, whether the grant strings turn golden handcuffs.
pitfalls: Calling it a second Judge with same authority—tone differs
Ignoring when it repeats Judge—then outcome is sticky
Forcing doom when it simply repeats caution
examples: Reconciler Carcer after Judge Laetitia—party then cage of responsibility
Caput after Cauda—new job offer after layoff season
